data.title: Molecular Partners and Orano Med Dose First Patients in MP0712 Phase 1/2a Trial
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Molecular Partners ( (MOLN) ) has provided an announcement.

On July 2, 2026, Molecular Partners and Orano Med reported that the first patients have been dosed in a U.S. multicenter Phase 1/2a trial of MP0712, a DLL3-targeting Radio-DARPin carrying a lead‑212 payload. The program, conducted at five active sites and using a matched-pair imaging and therapy approach with 203Pb and 212Pb versions of MP0712, marks the clinical debut of Molecular Partners’ lead Radio-DARPin in small cell lung cancer and aggressive neuroendocrine tumors.

The initial cohort in the study is now fully recruited, with patients progressing to repeat dosing as investigators build the safety profile across up to four dose levels. Early readouts are expected within months and a fuller safety and efficacy dataset is planned for 2027, underscoring the partners’ intent to validate DLL3-targeted alpha therapy and to pave the way for a broader pipeline of Radio-DARPin and lead‑212-based oncology candidates.

By targeting DLL3, which is expressed in more than 85% of small cell lung cancer tumors and minimally in healthy tissues, MP0712 is designed to deliver high linear energy transfer alpha radiation directly to malignant cells while limiting off-target damage. Successful clinical validation would strengthen Molecular Partners’ and Orano Med’s positioning in the competitive radiopharmaceutical field and could open new treatment options in hard-to-treat neuroendocrine malignancies.

The most recent analyst rating on (MOLN) stock is a Buy
with a $12.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Molecular Partners stock,
see the MOLN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MOLN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MOLN is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial performance (no revenue, large losses, and substantial cash burn). Technicals are broadly neutral and do not meaningfully offset fundamentals, while valuation is constrained by negative earnings and the absence of a dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on MOLN stock,
click here.

More about Molecular Partners

Molecular Partners AG is a clinical-stage biotechnology company specializing in DARPin therapeutics, a novel class of custom-built protein drugs designed to tackle medical challenges that are hard to address with conventional modalities. The Swiss-U.S. group focuses on oncology, including targeted radiopharmaceuticals and next-generation immune cell engagers, and advances both proprietary and partnered programs across preclinical and clinical stages.

Orano Med, a subsidiary of the Orano Group, is a clinical-stage biotech focused on targeted alpha therapies using lead-212-based radioligand treatments. With manufacturing and R&D operations in France and the U.S., the company is building a pipeline of 212Pb-linked cancer therapies and expanding GMP capacity in North America and Europe to support broader clinical deployment.

Molecular Partners leverages DARPin properties such as high affinity, specificity, small size, and tunable half-life to build multispecific and highly stable drug candidates. Its Radio-DARPin platform aims to improve radioligand therapy by enhancing tumor uptake and reducing kidney accumulation through half-life extension and surface engineering, positioning the firm as a technology leader in next-generation radiopharmaceuticals.

Average Trading Volume: 5,647

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $154.7M

Find detailed analytics on MOLN stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Star Fashion Culture Sets July 13 EGM to Revise Share Allotment and Approve New U.S. Equity Offering
data.description:
4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An announcement from Star Fashion Culture Holdings Ltd. Class A ( (STFS) ) is now available.

Star Fashion Culture Holdings Limited has called an extraordinary general meeting of shareholders to be held in Xiamen on July 13, 2026, with a record date of June 16, 2026, and has begun mailing proxy materials as of July 1, 2026. Holders of both Class A and Class B ordinary shares are invited to vote in person or by proxy on key resolutions that will shape the company’s future capital structure.

One major item seeks to revise previously approved terms for allotting up to 5,000,000 unissued Class B shares to Xingji Zhangpingting Limited, shifting the determination of consideration to a board-selected independent valuation institution over a three-year window from February 24, 2026. Another proposal would authorize an offering of up to 10,000,000 new Class A shares, the filing of a Form F-1 registration statement with the U.S. SEC, and broad powers for directors and officers to appoint placement agents, set pricing, and execute the necessary transaction documents, signaling a planned capital raise that could affect dilution, liquidity and the company’s market positioning.

More about Star Fashion Culture Holdings Ltd. Class A

Star Fashion Culture Holdings Limited is a China-based company headquartered in Xiamen, Fujian Province, and listed in the United States as a foreign private issuer. The group issues Class A and Class B ordinary shares and is positioning itself to access international capital markets through U.S. securities registrations and equity offerings.
